entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A legume species (Cicer arietinum L.) being studied for its root system architecture.

entities.name: ROOT SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE
entities.description: The spatial configuration of root systems, influencing plant functions and yield.

entities.name: RHIZOBOX-BASED STUDY
entities.description: A method using rhizoboxes to observe and study root system architecture.

entities.name: STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE
entities.description: A set of step-by-step instructions to achieve uniformity in phenotyping and screening.

entities.name: PHENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of measuring physical traits of an organism.

entities.name: SCREENING
entities.description: The process of identifying specific traits or genotypes within a population.

entities.name: PRE-BREEDING
entities.description: The stage of crop improvement focused on introducing new genetic diversity.

entities.name: NUTRIENT UPTAKE
entities.description: The process by which plants absorb minerals from the soil.

entities.name: WATER UPTAKE
entities.description: The process by which plants absorb water from the soil.

entities.name: ANCHORING
entities.description: The ability of the root system to hold the plant firmly in the soil.

entities.name: MECHANICAL SUPPORT
entities.description: The structural support provided by roots to the plant body.

entities.name: PLANT GENOTYPE
entities.description: The genetic makeup of an individual plant.

entities.name: ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: External factors that influence the growth and development of the plant.

entities.name: SOIL OPACITY
entities.description: The quality of soil that prevents light from passing through, hindering root observation.

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT PROGRAM
entities.description: A systematic effort to improve crop yield and quality through breeding.
